description: Set the value of wall attributes. This command is a synonym for the wall initialize command. Individual attributes can be listed with the wall list command and all attributes can be visualized. The optional vertex keyword can be used with the velocity keywords to set the vertex velocities for vertices in the specified range. The optional facet keyword can be used with the conveyor velocity keywords to set the facet conveyor velocities for facets in the specified range. Note: Be aware of the distinction between attributes and properties! The tutorial example 'Attributes and Properties' discusses this issue in detail.

modifiers.name: add
modifiers.description: Add f to the existing value

modifiers.name: gradient
modifiers.description: Apply a linear gradient in each of the axes directions starting at the origin

modifiers.name: gradient-x
modifiers.description: Apply a linear gradient in the x-direction starting at the origin

modifiers.name: gradient-y
modifiers.description: Apply a linear gradient in the y-direction starting at the origin

modifiers.name: gradient-z
modifiers.description: Apply a linear gradient in the z-direction starting at the origin (3D ONLY)

modifiers.name: multiply
modifiers.description: Multiply the existing value by f

modifiers.name: replace
modifiers.description: Replace the existing value with f

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: Wall.set_*() methods (e.g., Wall.set_vel(), Wall.set_spin(), Wall.set_pos(), Wall.set_disp(), Wall.set_rotation_center()) - requires iteration over wall range

keywords.name: cutoff-angle
keywords.description: Set the cutoff angle (an angle in degrees) for walls. By default, the cutoff angle is 0. This angle controls contact state information propagation.

keywords.name: displacement
keywords.description: Accumulated wall displacement vector as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: displacement-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the accumulated wall displacement as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: displacement-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the accumulated wall displacement as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: displacement-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the accumulated wall displacement as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: euler
keywords.description: Current orientation of Euler angles following the X,Y,Z convention (e.g., rotation about the \(x\) -axis followed by rotation about the y ’-axis followed by rotation about the z ’‘-axis) in degrees. The orientation is updated only when orientation tracking has been enabled (see model orientation-tracking command). When active, the current clump orientation can be visualized.

keywords.name: euler-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -euler angle (in degrees) of the current wall orientation. See the euler keyword for further details.

keywords.name: euler-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -euler angle (in degrees) of the current wall orientation. See the euler keyword for further details.

keywords.name: euler-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -euler angle (in degrees) of the current wall orientation. See the euler keyword for further details.

keywords.name: fragment
keywords.description: Fragment ID (see “ Fragment ”).

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: Wall location vector. This corresponds to the average position of all vertices. The center of rotation is not modified.

keywords.name: position-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the wall location. This corresponds to the average position of all vertices. The center of rotation is not modified.

keywords.name: position-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the wall location. This corresponds to the average position of all vertices. The center of rotation is not modified.

keywords.name: position-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the wall location. This corresponds to the average position of all vertices. The center of rotation is not modified.

keywords.name: rotation-center
keywords.description: Set the center of rotation for walls in the range. This is the position about which rotations occur when an angular velocity is supplied to a wall. The center of rotation translates with the wall if a translational velocity is supplied. The default center of rotation is the origin.

keywords.name: rotation-center-x
keywords.description: Set the \(x\) -component of the center of rotation for walls in the range. This is the position about which rotations occur when an angular velocity is supplied to a wall. The center of rotation translates with the wall if a translational velocity is supplied. The default center of rotation is the origin.

keywords.name: rotation-center-y
keywords.description: Set the \(y\) -component of the center of rotation for walls in the range. This is the position about which rotations occur when an angular velocity is supplied to a wall. The center of rotation translates with the wall if a translational velocity is supplied. The default center of rotation is the origin.

keywords.name: rotation-center-z
keywords.description: Set the \(z\) -component of the center of rotation for walls in the range. This is the position about which rotations occur when an angular velocity is supplied to a wall. The center of rotation translates with the wall if a translational velocity is supplied. The default center of rotation is the origin.

keywords.name: spin
keywords.description: The wall angular velocity in radians per second. Walls rotate around their center of rotation.

keywords.name: spin-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the wall angular velocity in radians per second. Walls rotate around their center of rotation.

keywords.name: spin-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the wall angular velocity in radians per second. Walls rotate around their center of rotation.

keywords.name: spin-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the wall angular velocity in radians per second. Walls rotate around their center of rotation.

keywords.name: velocity
keywords.description: Wall translational velocity vector. If given with the vertex keyword, then this corresponds to the vertex velocity and the range applies to vertices.

keywords.name: velocity-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the wall translational velocity. If given with the vertex keyword, then this corresponds to the \(x\) -component of the vertex velocity and the range applies to vertices.

keywords.name: velocity-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the wall translational velocity. If given with the vertex keyword, then this corresponds to the \(y\) -component of the vertex velocity and the range applies to vertices.

keywords.name: velocity-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the wall translational velocity. If given with the vertex keyword, then this corresponds to the \(z\) -component of the vertex velocity and the range applies to vertices.

keywords.name: velocity-conveyor
keywords.description: Set the facet conveyor velocity vector. If given with the facet keyword, then the range applies to facets. Otherwise the conveyor velocity is set for all facets in each wall in the specified range. The conveyor velocity is a fictitious velocity that is added to the wall velocity when calculating the relative velocity between a facet and another piece. A conveyor velocity cannot be active when vertex velocities are active. Setting a nonzero conveyor velocity allows one to simulate conveyance of balls or clumps along facets without moving the facets.

keywords.name: velocity-conveyor-x
keywords.description: Set the \(x\) -component of the facet conveyor velocity. If given with the facet keyword, then the range applies to facets. Otherwise the conveyor velocity is set for all facets in each wall in the specified range. The conveyor velocity is a fictitious velocity that is added to the wall velocity when calculating the relative velocity between a facet and another piece. A conveyor velocity cannot be active when vertex velocities are active. Setting a nonzero conveyor velocity allows one to simulate conveyance of balls or clumps along facets without moving the facets.

keywords.name: velocity-conveyor-y
keywords.description: Set the \(y\) -component of the facet conveyor velocity. If given with the facet keyword, then the range applies to facets. Otherwise the conveyor velocity is set for all facets in each wall in the specified range. The conveyor velocity is a fictitious velocity that is added to the wall velocity when calculating the relative velocity between a facet and another piece. A conveyor velocity cannot be active when vertex velocities are active. Setting a nonzero conveyor velocity allows one to simulate conveyance of balls or clumps along facets without moving the facets.

keywords.name: velocity-conveyor-z
keywords.description: Set the \(z\) -component of the facet conveyor velocity. If given with the facet keyword, then the range applies to facets. Otherwise the conveyor velocity is set for all facets in each wall in the specified range. The conveyor velocity is a fictitious velocity that is added to the wall velocity when calculating the relative velocity between a facet and another piece. A conveyor velocity cannot be active when vertex velocities are active. Setting a nonzero conveyor velocity allows one to simulate conveyance of balls or clumps along facets without moving the facets.

keywords.description: Add f to the existing value.

keywords.name: kwd:gradient
keywords.description: Apply a linear gradient in each of the axes directions starting at the origin.

keywords.name: kwd:gradient-x
keywords.description: Apply a linear gradient in the \(x\) -direction starting at the origin.

keywords.name: kwd:gradient-y

keywords.name: kwd:gradient-z
keywords.description: Apply a linear gradient in the \(z\) -direction starting at the origin.

keywords.name: kwd:multiply
keywords.description: Multiply the existing value by f .

keywords.name: kwd:replace
keywords.description: Replace the existing value with f .
